examples.title: Synchronous Replication Commit
examples.description: Demonstrates how synchronous replication commits transactions
examples.code:
FUNCTION sync_commit(transaction):
 BEGIN
 // Write to master
 write_to_master(transaction)
 
 // Send to all replicas in parallel
 FOR EACH replica IN replicas:
 parallel_send(replica, transaction)
 
 // Wait for all acknowledgments
 FOR EACH replica IN replicas:
 ack = wait_for_ack(replica)
 IF NOT ack THEN:
 ROLLBACK transaction
 RAISE "Replication failed"
 
 // All confirmed, commit
 COMMIT transaction
 RETURN success
END
examples.explanation: Shows synchronous replication waiting for all replicas before committing, ensuring strong consistency but adding latency

examples.title: Asynchronous Replication Process
examples.description: Demonstrates asynchronous replication with replication lag
examples.code:
FUNCTION async_commit(transaction):
 BEGIN
 // Immediately write to master
 write_to_master(transaction)
 COMMIT transaction
 
 // Queue for replication (non-blocking)
 queue_for_replication(transaction)
 
 RETURN success
END

FUNCTION replication_worker():
 BEGIN
 WHILE running:
 transaction = dequeue(replication_queue)
 FOR EACH replica IN replicas:
 send_to_replica(replica, transaction)
 mark_replicated(transaction)
 END
END
examples.explanation: Shows non-blocking commit in asynchronous replication, with separate worker for propagating changes to replicas
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for commit, O(n) for propagation

examples.title: Automatic Failover Procedure
examples.description: Shows automatic failover from failed master to replica
examples.code:
FUNCTION detect_failure():
 BEGIN
 WHILE monitoring:
 IF NOT ping(master) THEN:
 IF retry_count > threshold THEN:
 trigger_failover()
 wait(heartbeat_interval)
 END

FUNCTION trigger_failover():
 BEGIN
 // Stop replication on candidates
 FOR EACH candidate IN candidates:
 STOP_REPLICATION(candidate)
 
 // Promote most up-to-date replica
 best_replica = select_best_candidate(candidates)
 
 // Make it new master
 PROMOTE_TO_MASTER(best_replica)
 
 // Reconfigure other replicas
 FOR EACH replica IN other_replicas:
 CHANGE_MASTER_TO(best_replica, replica)
 
 // Update application connections
 UPDATE_LOAD_BALANCER(best_replica)
 
 ALERT_ADMIN("Failover completed")
END
examples.explanation: Demonstrates automatic failover mechanism including failure detection, replica promotion, and reconfiguration

examples.title: Conflict Resolution - Last Write Wins
examples.description: Demonstrates one method of resolving write conflicts in multi-master replication
examples.code:
FUNCTION resolve_conflict(local_write, remote_write):
 BEGIN
 // Compare timestamps
 IF local_write.timestamp > remote_write.timestamp THEN:
 RETURN local_write
 ELSE:
 RETURN remote_write
 END

FUNCTION apply_replicated_write(write_record):
 BEGIN
 current_data = READ(write_record.key)
 
 IF current_data.last_modified == write_record.parent_version THEN:
 // No conflict, apply directly
 WRITE(write_record.key, write_record.value)
 ELSE:
 // Conflict detected, resolve
 resolved = resolve_conflict(current_data, write_record)
 WRITE(write_record.key, resolved.value)
 LOG_CONFLICT(current_data, write_record, resolved)
 END
examples.explanation: Shows timestamp-based conflict resolution in master-master replication, automatically choosing most recent write
